message:
feat(responsiveness): flip default calibration mode to Conformal (post-flip behavior)

Governor calibration mode now defaults to `CalibrationMode::Conformal`
instead of `CalibrationMode::Static`. Every host gets the adaptive
per-host threshold with a finite-sample coverage guarantee by default;
the legacy static thresholds are now opt-in via
`CASS_RESPONSIVENESS_CALIBRATION=static`.

Before:
  match env("CASS_RESPONSIVENESS_CALIBRATION") {
      Some("conformal") => CalibrationMode::Conformal,
      _ => CalibrationMode::Static,          // legacy default
  }

After:
  match env("CASS_RESPONSIVENESS_CALIBRATION") {
      Some("static") => CalibrationMode::Static,
      _ => CalibrationMode::Conformal,       // new default
  }

Why this is a "flip", not a "switch": the conformal mode has been
shadow-running for long enough that the calibration telemetry shows
stable per-host bounds on real workloads. Sticking with static
thresholds on any host >16 GB was producing strictly worse coverage
than the conformal path. The old env-gated rollout shape is preserved
so rollback is a single env var on any host that hits an unexpected
regression.

Same file: collapse a now-trivial formatter-driven wrap around
`next_capacity(prev, streak, &snapshot, &effective_cfg)` and similar
cosmetic one-liner normalizations; test assertions adjusted to the
same function shape. No behavior change in those lines, just
tidy-up caused by rustfmt on neighbouring hunks.

Test suite retains full coverage of both modes — the
"conformal must emit calibration telemetry" assertion continues to
hold because the mode is still reachable; it just happens to be the
default now.
